name: Blinded
summary: You can't see, and all terrain is difficult terrain to you.
description: You can't see. All normal terrain is difficult terrain to you. You can't detect anything using vision. You automatically critically fail Perception checks that require you to be able to see, and if vision is your only precise sense, you take a -4 status penalty to Perception checks. You are immune to visual effects. Blinded overrides dazzled.

properties.effect: Can't see, all terrain is difficult terrain, -4 to Perception if vision is only precise sense, immune to visual effects

name: Broken
summary: An item that has taken enough damage that it can't function normally.
description: An item with the broken condition can't be used for its normal purpose. It still imposes penalties for carrying it. An item becomes broken when its Hit Points drop to its Broken Threshold or lower. Items can be repaired using the Repair action from the Crafting skill.

properties.effect: Item can't be used for its normal function, still imposes carrying penalties

name: Clumsy
summary: Your movements become clumsy and imprecise.
description: Your movements become clumsy and imprecise. Clumsy always includes a value. You take a status penalty equal to the condition value to Dexterity-based checks and DCs, including AC, Reflex saves, ranged attack rolls, and skill checks using Acrobatics, Stealth, and Thievery.

properties.effect: Status penalty equal to value on Dexterity-based checks and DCs, including AC and Reflex saves

name: Confused
summary: You attack indiscriminately, unable to tell friend from foe.
description: You don't have your wits about you, and you attack indiscriminately. You can't take reactions. You use all your actions to Strike or cast offensive cantrips, though the GM determines targets randomly. If you have no valid targets, you babble incoherently, wasting your actions. On each turn, before you act, you can attempt a DC 11 flat check to recover and end the condition.

properties.effect: Attack indiscriminately, can't take reactions, GM determines targets randomly, DC 11 flat check each turn to end

name: Dazzled
summary: Your eyes are overstimulated, making it hard to see clearly.
description: Your eyes are overstimulated. If vision is your only precise sense, all creatures and objects are concealed from you. If you have a precise sense besides vision, you can use that to observe a creature or object and remove the concealed condition.

properties.effect: All creatures and objects are concealed if vision is your only precise sense

name: Deafened
summary: You can't hear and take penalties to auditory-related checks.
description: You can't hear. You automatically critically fail Perception checks that require you to hear. You take a -2 status penalty to Perception checks for initiative and checks that involve sound but also other senses. If you perform an action that has the auditory trait, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is lost.

properties.effect: Can't hear, auto-fail hearing checks, -2 Perception, DC 5 flat check for auditory actions

name: Doomed
summary: Your death approaches more quickly; your dying value increases.
description: A powerful force has claimed your soul, pulling you closer to death. Doomed always includes a value. The dying value at which you die is reduced by your doomed value. If your maximum dying value is reduced to 0, you instantly die. When you die, you're no longer doomed. Your doomed value decreases by 1 each time you get a full night's rest.

properties.effect: Dying threshold reduced by doomed value; if reduced to 0, instant death

name: Drained
summary: Life force has been sucked from you, reducing HP and Constitution.
description: When a creature successfully drains you of blood, life force, or some other essence, you become drained. Drained always includes a value. You take a status penalty equal to your drained value on Constitution-based checks. You also lose a number of Hit Points equal to your level times the drained value, and your maximum Hit Points are reduced by the same amount.

properties.effect: Status penalty to Con checks, lose HP equal to level × value, max HP reduced by same amount

name: Dying
summary: You are bleeding out or otherwise at death's door.
description: You are bleeding out or otherwise at death's door. While you have this condition, you are unconscious. Dying always includes a value, and if it ever reaches dying 4 (or 4 minus your doomed value), you die. When you're dying, you must attempt a recovery check at the start of each of your turns to determine whether you get better or worse.

properties.effect: Unconscious, recovery check each turn; die at dying 4 (reduced by doomed value)

name: Enfeebled
description: You are physically weakened. Enfeebled always includes a value. You take a status penalty equal to the condition value to Strength-based rolls and DCs, including Strength-based melee attack rolls, Strength-based damage rolls, and Athletics checks.

properties.effect: Status penalty to Strength-based rolls and DCs, including melee attacks and damage

name: Frightened
summary: Fear causes you to take penalties to checks and DCs.
description: You are gripped by fear and struggle to control your nerves. Frightened always includes a value. You take a status penalty equal to this value to all your checks and DCs. Unless specified otherwise, at the end of each of your turns, the value of your frightened condition decreases by 1.

properties.effect: Status penalty equal to value on all checks and DCs, decreases by 1 at end of each turn

name: Grabbed
summary: A creature has you in its grip, restricting your movement.
description: You are held in place by another creature, giving you the flat-footed and immobilized conditions. If you attempt a manipulate action while grabbed, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or it is lost; roll the check after spending the action, but before any effects are applied.

properties.effect: Flat-footed and immobilized, DC 5 flat check for manipulate actions

name: Paralyzed
summary: Your body is frozen in place, unable to act.
description: Your body is frozen in place. You have the flat-footed condition and can't act except to Recall Knowledge and use actions that require only the use of your mind (as determined by the GM). Your senses still function, but only in the areas you can perceive without moving your body.

properties.effect: Flat-footed, can't act except mental actions, senses still function

name: Sickened
summary: You feel ill and take penalties to most checks.
description: You feel ill. Sickened always includes a value. You take a status penalty equal to this value on all your checks and DCs. You can't willingly ingest anything — including elixirs and potions — while sickened. You can spend a single action retching in an attempt to recover, which lets you immediately attempt a Fortitude save against the DC of the effect that made you sickened.

properties.effect: Status penalty to all checks and DCs, can't ingest items, can spend action to attempt Fortitude save to recover

name: Stunned
summary: You've become senseless and can't act.
description: You've become senseless. You can't act while stunned. Stunned usually includes a value, which indicates how many total actions you lose, possibly over multiple turns, from being stunned. Each time you regain actions, reduce the number of actions you regain by your stunned value, then reduce your stunned value by the number of actions you lost.

properties.effect: Can't act, lose actions equal to stunned value across turns

name: Unconscious
summary: You are sleeping or have been knocked out.
description: You are sleeping, or you've been knocked out. You can't act. You take a -4 status penalty to AC, Perception, and Reflex saves, and you have the blinded and flat-footed conditions. When you gain this condition, you fall prone and drop items you are holding unless the effect states otherwise.

properties.effect: -4 to AC/Perception/Reflex, blinded, flat-footed, prone, drop held items
